name: atlas
description: Self-configuring multi-agent coding architect with automatic self-improvement (persistent memory, auto-skill creation, skill lifecycle curation, context optimization), 184 skills (28 top-level + 156 armada across 11 departments; the 28 top-level are 11 Greek-mythology core skills: atlas-metis, atlas-hephaestus, atlas-ariadne, atlas-athena, atlas-argus, atlas-chronos, atlas-hermes, atlas-odysseus, atlas-nestor, atlas-armada, atlas-olympus; plus 17 task skills such as atlas-feature, atlas-debug, atlas-doctor, atlas-validate, atlas-refactor, atlas-harden, atlas-launch), 23 agents (12 core + 11 armada department), 10 hooks (session boot, prompt optimizer, bash advisor, format-after-edit, dispatch tripwire, completion gate, memory capture, auto-skill, self-improvement nudge, session-transcript ingest), and the atlas-metis method (evidence before any done claim). The atlas self-improvement system provides: persistent memory at ~/.atlas/memory/ (MEMORY.md + PROJECT.md), automatic skill creation from session transcripts at ~/.atlas/skills/, skill lifecycle curation (stale/archive/pin), and context optimization that disables unused skills/agents to reduce token cost by up to 70%. Deployed automatically via atlas-hephaestus setup. All data stored under ~/.atlas/ branding.
